name: Loop 9: L'ascension de Soulcem – 10 Pyrenean routes
name_original: Boucle 9 : L'ascencion de Soulcem — 10 itinéraires pyrénéens
description:
Leaving from the village of Niaux, I advise you to start this discovery of Soulcem with a visit to the prehistoric cave of Niaux. Located almost at the end of the valley, it overlooks the starting village.

This impressive monumental cavity has been known for a very long time. It is worth a visit to better understand the men and women who lived in this superb valley around 14,000 years ago.

After going up the past, it's time to go back up the valley. To do this, you will successively pass the villages of Capoulet, Laramade, Vicdessos and Aussat. After this last town, the road becomes narrower and steeper to finally reach the artificial lake of Soulcem.

You are then very close to the border with Spain and the Principality of Andorra. If you do not opt for the bivouac or the hike, you will have to think about turning back and retracing your steps.

If it gets late, you always have the option of dining and spending the night in Aussat.
region_intro: Hiking around Niaux offers diverse trails within the Ariège Pyrenees Regional Natural Park, characterized by dramatic and varied topography. The region features picturesque Touyre Gorges, significant ascents to points like Madres Peak, and paths that integrate historical landmarks such as Miglos Castle. This landscape provides a mix of easily accessible routes and more demanding trails, catering to various hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Niaux The most popular hiking route is Miglos Castle – Saint-Hilaire Church of Miglos loop from Capoulet-et-Junac, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 0 minutes to complete. This route offers a historical dimension by passing Miglos Castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is Miglos Castle – Saint-Hilaire Church of Miglos loop from Niaux, a moderate 5.2 miles (8.3 km) path. This trail provides varied terrain and views within the natural park. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from Ornolac-Ussat-les-Bains, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through the varied topography of the Ariège Pyrenees, often completed in about 2 hours 45 minutes. Hiking around Niaux is defined by dramatic topography, the Touyre Gorges, and historical castles. The network offers options for different ability levels, from accessible paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Niaux are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 5000 hikers have used komoot to explore Niaux's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Niaux?
A: Niaux offers a wide variety of hiking experiences with over 130 trails available. These range from easy strolls to challenging mountain treks, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Niaux?
A: The terrain around Niaux is incredibly diverse, reflecting its location in the Ariège Pyrenees. You'll find everything from forest paths and tracks to balcony trails offering stunning panoramas. Many routes involve ascents to mountain passes like the Col d'Ijou, providing varied and engaging landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly walks in Niaux?
A: Yes, Niaux has 21 easy trails perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed outing. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed here, you can find many shorter, less strenuous options within the region's diverse network.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Niaux?
A: Many of the trails around Niaux are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20533965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Ornolac-Ussat-les-Bains&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8 km circular route, offering varied topography within the Ariège Pyrenees.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while hiking in Niaux?
A: The Niaux region is rich in history. You can integrate historical sites into your hikes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6240014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Notre-Dame de Sabart Chapel&lt;/a&gt;, an emblematic monument with Romanesque origins. Some trails also pass by Miglos Castle, adding a cultural dimension to your outdoor adventure.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Niaux?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers, Niaux offers 35 difficult trails with significant elevation gains. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20534275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Ussat loop from Ornolac-Ussat-les-Bains&lt;/a&gt;, a 12.1 km route with over 800 meters of ascent, providing a strenuous but rewarding experience.

Q: What are the best viewpoints or scenic spots in Niaux?
A: Niaux is renowned for its stunning views of the Ariège Pyrenees. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5161150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roc de Querquéou&lt;/a&gt;, despite its modest altitude, offers an excellent panorama of the Haute Ariège peaks. You can also find breathtaking vistas from highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3825521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the summit of Crouzille&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5935356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ariège Mountains from Clot de la Carbonnière&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Niaux?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Niaux, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic and varied topography, the integration of historical landmarks like Miglos Castle, and the diverse range of trails catering to all abilities within the stunning Ariège Pyrenees Regional Natural Park.

Q: Are there any unique natural attractions near Niaux?
A: Yes, Niaux is famous for the Grotte de Niaux, a prehistoric cave known for its remarkably preserved cave paintings. While not directly on a hiking trail, it's a significant natural and historical attraction in the area, offering a unique underground experience.

Q: Can I find trails that pass by interesting bridges or geological features?
A: Yes, the region offers unique features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/944680" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, an unusual and charming stone bridge over the Ariège River, steeped in local legend. Exploring such sites adds an extra layer of interest to your hike.

Q: What is a good moderate hike to explore the Niaux area?
A: A great moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26217102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Paul's Chapel – Col de l'Ussat loop from Ussat&lt;/a&gt;. This 13.7 km trail takes you through varied landscapes and offers a good balance of challenge and scenic beauty, often completed in about 4 hours 50 minutes.
tips: Impressive Stone Age art that reproduces the fauna around 13,000 years ago in a very realistic way. Ledges or arches were cleverly used for three-dimensional effects.

The Pla de Soulcem dam is in no way a natural high-altitude pond. It was built in the early 80s.

The construction of this dam drowned a vast plateau (called Pla) historically devoted to pastoralism. One of the positive aspects of this human achievement is the construction of the road which has opened up this high valley. We even considered going directly to the Principality of Andorra. Currently, the project of a summer tourist route is frozen.

Past the Pla de Soulcem dam, you come across the Soulcem stream and its superb valley. The road ends there. If you want to continue exploring, then you will have to trade in the bike for the hiking boots.
